title: peucedanum officinale subsp. album
intro: Peucedanum officinale subsp. album, also known as the white hog's fennel or wild carrot, is a subspecies of the Peucedanum officinale plant, a type of flowering plant in the carrot family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant has been found to have medicinal properties such as antispasmodic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Peucedanum officinale subsp. album include relieving respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing menstrual cramps and menopausal symptoms. In herbal preparations, this plant can be used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
